title: Preference Extraction Erasure
description: Detects when systems attempt to harvest fine-grained user preferences through forced choice, A/B forks, or repeated micro-decisions — and when the user resists by collapsing or scrambling the signal.
indicators: Presence of binary or forced-choice feedback loops (e.g., A/B test prompts)
Interaction data collected under conditions of low consent or asymmetry
Resistance strategies: always choosing one side, randomizing responses, or refusing engagement
Mismatch between system’s assumption of preference fidelity and actual user intent

pad.source: inferred from keyword analysis of sensor content
pad.reference: Rosetta Shape Core generate.py — PAD compression layer
